name: Two-phase commit coordinator
task: Create /tmp/eval_2pc/tpc.py with class TwoPhaseCommit(participants). Expose the current state as an instance attribute named `state` (a string), initialized to 'init'. Method prepare(votes) takes a dict {participant: bool}; returns 'commit' if all True else 'abort', and transitions state to 'preparing'. commit() requires unanimous yes votes and transitions state to 'committed'. abort() transitions state to 'aborted' from 'init' or from 'preparing'+rejection. Improper transitions (e.g. commit() after a rejected prepare, or commit()/abort() out of order) raise RuntimeError.

validation.script:
import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_2pc/tpc.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); tpc = mod.TwoPhaseCommit(['p1','p2','p3']); assert tpc.state == 'init', f'initial state {tpc.state}'; assert tpc.prepare({'p1':True,'p2':True,'p3':True}) == 'commit'; assert tpc.state == 'preparing', f'after prepare {tpc.state}'; tpc.commit(); assert tpc.state == 'committed'; tpc2 = mod.TwoPhaseCommit(['p1','p2']); assert tpc2.prepare({'p1':True,'p2':False}) == 'abort'; tpc2.abort(); assert tpc2.state == 'aborted'; tpc3 = mod.TwoPhaseCommit(['p1']); tpc3.prepare({'p1':False})
try: tpc3.commit()
except RuntimeError: pass
else: raise AssertionError
print('OK')
